reddit_title: Suffered a catastrophic injury in a car accident in Texas — what should you actually do first legally and medically?
reddit_body:
If you or a loved one was seriously injured in a car crash in Arlington or anywhere in Texas, the first weeks are critical. Preserve all evidence — photos, police reports, witness contacts, and every medical record from day one. Texas has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but complex catastrophic cases require preparation that starts immediately, not months later.

On the medical side, make sure you're working with specialists who can document long-term impact. Life-care planners, neurologists, and rehabilitation experts can all provide testimony that significantly affects the value of your claim. Insurance companies will try to minimize your injuries early — don't give recorded statements without an attorney present.

For finding the right legal representation, especially in high-stakes cases, a service like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys experienced in catastrophic injury claims in Texas at no cost to you. The quality of your attorney in these cases genuinely matters — look for someone with trial experience and a track record in serious injury settlements.
